message:
Refine TUI help overlay with structured sections and detailed guidance

This commit improves the in-app help overlay (triggered by '?') with a
more structured presentation that groups related commands and provides
clearer context for each feature.

Help overlay improvements:
- Section-based organization replaces flat bullet list:
  - Search: explains live-search behavior and query focus hotkey
  - Filters: documents all filter dimensions (agent, workspace, time)
    with visual pill indicator reference
  - Navigate: consolidates movement keys with pagination shortcuts
  - Detail: describes multi-tab view (Messages, Snippets, Raw)
  - Actions: groups utility commands (open in editor, theme toggle)
  - Quit: clearly highlights exit paths (q or esc)

Presentation enhancements:
- Span-based styling with theme colors for section headers
- Consistent typography using palette.title() for visual hierarchy
- Prose descriptions instead of terse abbreviations
  (e.g., "type to live-search" vs just "/: search")
- Pipe separators for related alternatives (j/k or arrows)
- Contextual explanations (pills show below the bar, role colors, etc.)

User experience benefits:
- New users can understand full feature set at a glance
- Grouped layout makes it easier to find specific command categories
- Descriptive text reduces ambiguity (what does "Tab" do exactly?)
- Visual consistency with rest of TUI (same color scheme)

This complements the multi-mode TUI introduced in the prior commit by
making all features discoverable without external documentation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
